sources.title: Three.js GLTFLoader + DRACOLoader — Official Documentation (r183)
sources.summary: GLB/GLTF 캐릭터 모델 로딩. r183에서 'empty groups with multiple scene references' 수정. GLTF result: scene, animations[], cameras[], asset, parser. DRACOLoader로 80-90% geometry 압축 가능. import path: three/addons/loaders/GLTFLoader.js

claims.text: GLTFLoader.loadAsync(url) returns Promise<GLTF> with scene (Group), animations (AnimationClip[]), cameras, asset metadata. r183 fully compatible.
DRACOLoader WASM decoder (gstatic.com CDN) compresses geometry 80-90%. CPU decode cost offsets network savings for small models (<100KB).
GLTF scene.traverse + isMesh pattern for castShadow/receiveShadow setup. Standard integration pattern for all GLTF models.

sources.title: Three.js MeshPhysicalMaterial — Complete PBR Property Reference (r183)
sources.summary: 전체 PBR feature set: metalness, roughness, clearcoat, iridescence, sheen, transmission, ior, anisotropy, dispersion. r183에서 clearcoat support for rectangular area lights 추가. 'Higher performance cost per pixel than other materials.'

claims.text: MeshPhysicalMaterial supports clearcoat (0-1), iridescence (thin-film, IOR 1.0-2.333, thickness 100-400nm), sheen (fabric), transmission (physical transparency with IOR-based refraction), anisotropy, dispersion. All disabled by default.
Each enabled MeshPhysicalMaterial feature adds shader complexity. transmission is most expensive. For education devices: enable only needed features per entity type.
Spacesuit visor: clearcoat(1.0) + iridescence(0.3) + envMap. Spaceship hull: metalness(0.8) + roughness(0.3) + clearcoat(0.5). These combinations are GPU-feasible per-entity.

sources.title: Three.js AnimationMixer + Skeletal Animation System (r183)
sources.summary: AnimationClip (data) + AnimationMixer (controller) + AnimationAction (per-clip control). GLTF animations loaded via gltf.animations[]. crossFadeTo/crossFadeFrom for smooth transitions. mixer.update(delta) must be called every frame. r183: BezierInterpolant + DEVTOOLS access.

claims.text: AnimationMixer: one per animated model. clipAction(clip).play() starts animation. crossFadeTo(action, 0.5, true) blends smoothly. Weight 0-1 controls influence.
For 10-50 animated characters, each requires separate AnimationMixer instance. Clock.getDelta() should be shared (not per-mixer). CPU-bound per-mixer update.
GLTF animation clips: idle, walk, board, celebrate, error — all can be embedded in single GLB file. AnimationClip.findByName(clips, 'idle') for named lookup.

sources.title: Three.js PMREMGenerator + Environment Maps — PBR Lighting Setup (r183)
sources.summary: PMREMGenerator converts equirectangular HDR/EXR to pre-filtered mipmap for roughness-based PBR sampling. scene.environment auto-applies to all MeshStandard/PhysicalMaterial. ACESFilmicToneMapping recommended. r183: PMREM cache fix.

claims.text: Complete PBR env setup: renderer.outputColorSpace=SRGBColorSpace, toneMapping=ACESFilmicToneMapping, toneMappingExposure=1.8. RGBELoader for .hdr, EXRLoader for .exr.
scene.environment = pmremGenerator.fromEquirectangular(texture).texture — automatically applies PBR lighting to ALL MeshStandard/PhysicalMaterial in scene. One-time GPU operation.
Poly Haven (polyhaven.com/hdris): CC0 license, hundreds of free HDRIs, 1K-8K resolution. 1K-2K sufficient for education apps (1-8MB).

sources.title: Three.js InstancedMesh — Batch Rendering (r183)
sources.summary: InstancedMesh renders multiple instances with single draw call. setMatrixAt/setColorAt per instance. CRITICAL: InstancedMesh does NOT support SkinnedMesh/skeletal animation. For animated characters, use separate SkinnedMesh + AnimationMixer per character.

claims.text: CRITICAL: InstancedMesh does NOT natively support SkinnedMesh/skeletal animation. No official API for instanced skeletal animation. Community workaround (bone texture atlas, PR #22667) not merged into core.
For 10-50 animated spacekid characters: use separate SkinnedMesh instances from GLTFLoader, each with own AnimationMixer. InstancedMesh only for static/identical objects (environmental props).
InstancedMesh performance: 10 instances — minimal benefit. 50+ — noticeable. 100+ — significant. Per-instance frustum culling NOT natively supported.

sources.title: Three.js DragControls — Drag-and-Drop Interaction (r183)
sources.summary: DragControls: drag objects in 3D. Constructor(objects[], camera, domElement). Events: hoveron/hoveroff/dragstart/drag/dragend. Touch support via touches property. Must disable OrbitControls during drag.

claims.text: DragControls events (dragstart/drag/dragend) provide event.object — the dragged Object3D. Integrates with GLTF-loaded models via recursive:true (default).
OrbitControls must be disabled during drag (orbitControls.enabled=false on dragstart, true on dragend). DragControls supports touch via touches:{ONE:PAN} property.
Custom raycasting for advanced hit testing: raycaster.setFromCamera(pointer, camera) + raycaster.intersectObjects(objects, true). Pointer events unify mouse+touch.

sources.title: Free 3D Model Sources for Education — Sketchfab/Mixamo/ReadyPlayerMe/PolyHaven
sources.summary: Sketchfab: 800K+ free models, CC licenses, auto GLB conversion. Mixamo: 2400+ free mo-cap animations, royalty-free, FBX→GLB conversion needed. Ready Player Me: GLB API, quality presets, morph targets. Poly Haven: CC0 HDRIs + models. KenShape: pixel→3D voxel tool ($3.99), GLTF export.

claims.text: Sketchfab: 800K+ free downloadable models, auto GLTF conversion, CC license filtering. Spaceship category: sketchfab.com/tags/spaceship. Character category: sketchfab.com/tags/character.
Mixamo: free royalty-free 2400+ mo-cap animations. Upload custom humanoid → auto-rig + apply animations. FBX output only → Blender/Mixamo2GLTF for GLB conversion.
Ready Player Me API: https://models.readyplayer.me/{id}.glb?quality=medium&meshLod=1. Quality presets (low/medium/high), configurable morph targets, Draco compression option.
Poly Haven: CC0 HDRIs (1K-8K), CC0 3D models, CC0 textures. Best source for environment maps in education apps — no attribution required.

sources.title: Cross-Reference Analysis — API Compatibility Matrix
sources.summary: Critical cross-references: (1) InstancedMesh + SkinnedMesh INCOMPATIBLE — animated characters must use separate SkinnedMesh. (2) GLTFLoader → AnimationMixer tightly coupled via gltf.animations[]. (3) PMREMGenerator → scene.environment auto-applies to MeshPhysicalMaterial. (4) DragControls works with GLTF models (recursive:true). (5) All 7 APIs fully compatible with r183.

claims.text: CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: InstancedMesh + SkinnedMesh are INCOMPATIBLE. For 10-50 animated spacekid characters: separate SkinnedMesh per character + shared geometry + individual AnimationMixer. Budget: ~25 characters at 60fps on mid-range GPU.
GLTFLoader.load() → gltf.scene (visual) + gltf.animations[] (clips) → AnimationMixer.clipAction(clip) — tightly coupled pipeline. Animation data MUST be embedded in GLB file.
Renderer config shared across Topics 2+4: outputColorSpace=SRGBColorSpace (r183 default), toneMapping=ACESFilmicToneMapping, toneMappingExposure=1.8. One-time global setup.

Existing mathcrew n8ao + postprocessing pipeline: MeshPhysicalMaterial is compatible with n8ao SSAO. No OverrideMaterialManager workaround needed (unlike troika-three-text). GLTF models participate in postprocessing naturally.

sources.title: miniplex v2.0.0 — Developer-Friendly ECS for TypeScript Games
sources.summary: miniplex ECS: World<Entity> manages typed entities. world.with(...components) returns archetypal query (iterable). world.add(entity), world.remove(entity). No built-in scheduling. onEntityAdded/onEntityRemoved events for lifecycle hooks. Entities are plain JS objects — direct property mutation OK for component values, use addComponent/removeComponent to add/remove components. ~2KB gzipped, MIT license, 2587 weekly downloads.

claims.text: miniplex v2.0.0 API: World<Entity> — world.add(entity), world.remove(entity), world.with(...components) → typed Query<Entity>. For...of iteration over queries. No built-in scheduling.

miniplex onEntityAdded/onEntityRemoved: reactive lifecycle hooks per query. Critical for Three.js: subscribe to onEntityRemoved to call geometry.dispose() + material.dispose() — miniplex.world.remove() does NOT auto-dispose Three.js resources.

miniplex bundle size: ~2KB gzipped (bundlephobia). Adds external dependency vs Factory+Registry zero-dep Map<>. Migration path: replace Map<ComponentType, CreateFn> with World<Entity> — query replaces registry lookup, system functions replace createFn dispatch.

sources.title: Pointer Events — Gesture Detection for 3D Games (MDN + Three.js forum)
sources.summary: PointerEvents API: setPointerCapture(pointerId) + releasePointerCapture on pointerdown/pointerup for reliable cross-device gesture tracking. Velocity calculation: delta position / elapsed time (px/sec). Flick threshold ~600 px/sec. For continuous gestures: capture pointer. OS edge conflict: iOS swipe-up (home) + Android nav gestures conflict with canvas touch events — requires CSS touch-action:none.

claims.text: PointerEvents best practice for gestures: setPointerCapture(e.pointerId) on pointerdown + releasePointerCapture on pointerup — ensures pointerMove events fire even when pointer leaves canvas. Applies to both mouse and touch.

Flick gesture velocity classification: delta_position / delta_time (px/sec). Threshold ~600 px/sec distinguishes drag from flick in game context. ZingTouch library provides configurable minVelocity parameter for swipe recognition.

OS edge gesture conflicts: iOS 15+ swipe-up triggers Dock/App Switcher; Android navigation gestures interfere with canvas touch. Mitigation: touch-action:none on renderer.domElement + viewport meta touch-action:none. High risk for H-6 at screen edges.

sources.title: DevCon 5 — Ontology Design Principles (§DC5-04, §DC5-05, §DC5-06)
sources.summary: 3-stage product journey (Golden Tables → Operational Decision-Making → AI-First). Four ontology design principles: DDD, DRY, Open-Closed, PECS. Primitives: Interfaces, Structs, Reducers, Derived properties, Object-backed link types.

claims.text: The 3-stage product journey applies to photonicRealism: (1) Golden Tables — entity definitions + material presets, (2) Operational — interaction systems + animation controllers, (3) AI-First — adaptive content delivery.
OCP (Open-Closed): PhotonicComponentFactory must be open for extension (new math concepts → new entity types) without modifying core factory code.
DDD: Entity names (SpaceshipEntity, SpacekidEntity) must match domain vocabulary. Teaching surface names must map 1:1 to ontology terms.
